title: gastrochilus retrocallus
intro: Gastrochilus retrocallus, also known as the Himalayan orchid, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant native to the Himalayan region. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating skin conditions, among others. In traditional medicine, Gastrochilus retrocallus is often prepared as a decoction or a paste, which is then used to treat various ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
